title: Stemmadenia obovata
taxonomy: Stemmadenia obovata belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. The genus Stemmadenia is part of the tribe Plumerieae, which consists of flowering plants native to Central and South America. The species obovata is likely a specific type within this genus. More research is needed to determine the exact classification within the family Apocynaceae.
uses: Stemmadenia obovata is known to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
